findings.title: IPv4-mapped IPv6 bypasses BLOCKED_HOSTS metadata check
findings.problem: The new IPv4-mapped IPv6 branch only re-tests the mapped address against is_link_local/is_loopback/is_unspecified/is_multicast, but NOT against BLOCKED_HOSTS. The BLOCKED_HOSTS set exists to block cloud metadata IPs, and 100.100.100.200 (Alibaba metadata) is neither link-local, loopback, unspecified, nor multicast. So a URL like http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]/ passes: the raw hostname '::ffff:100.100.100.200' is not in BLOCKED_HOSTS (line 77), and the mapped 100.100.100.200 is not caught by the four flag checks (line 87). Verified with ipaddress + urlparse: the address reaches the SSRF-protected metadata endpoint. This defeats the exact protection the mapped-address handling was added for. 169.254.169.254 (GCP/AWS metadata) happens to be caught because it is link-local, but the string-listed metadata IPs are not covered.
findings.fix_hint: also re-check str(mapped) against BLOCKED_HOSTS (and consider mapped.is_private) inside the ipv4_mapped branch
findings.why_it_matters: An attacker can reach cloud-metadata services listed in BLOCKED_HOSTS by wrapping the IPv4 metadata IP in an IPv4-mapped IPv6 literal, bypassing the SSRF guard this module is responsible for.
source_window: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:
findings.canonical_line_content: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:

findings.title: SSRF bypass via NAT64 and 6to4 IPv6-embedded-IPv4 addresses not unwrapped
findings.problem: The new check only unwraps IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (::ffff:a.b.c.d) via addr.ipv4_mapped. It does not unwrap NAT64 well-known-prefix addresses (64:ff9b::/96) or 6to4 addresses (2002::/16, via addr.sixtofour), both of which Python's ipaddress module classifies as is_global=True / not link-local/loopback/multicast on the wrapper address itself. An attacker can encode a blocked target (e.g. the cloud metadata IP 169.254.169.254, or 127.0.0.1) as 64:ff9b::a9fe:a9fe or 2002:a9fe:a9fe:: and the validator will accept it as a valid *arr URL, even though the embedded IPv4 address is one this function is explicitly trying to block.
findings.fix_hint: also check addr.sixtofour and manually unwrap the NAT64 well-known prefix (64:ff9b::/96, RFC 6052) by extracting the low 32 bits when addr is in that network, then apply the same is_link_local/is_loopback/is_unspecified/is_multicast/BLOCKED_HOSTS checks to the unwrapped IPv4 address in all three cases (ipv4_mapped, sixtofour, NAT64).
findings.why_it_matters: This function exists specifically to prevent SSRF against cloud metadata services (169.254.169.254 is in BLOCKED_HOSTS) and loopback/link-local targets. Since the underlying HTTP client connects to the literal IP once validation passes, an attacker who can set this URL can reach internal-only endpoints by wrapping the target IP in an alternate IPv6 transition encoding, defeating the purpose of this new hardening pass.
source_window: # Check IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (e.g., ::ffff:127.0.0.1) per D-10
if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:

findings.title: Legacy numeric IPv4 forms bypass the address block
findings.problem: The validator only applies blocked-address checks when `ipaddress.ip_address(hostname)` succeeds, then treats every `ValueError` as a safe hostname. I verified `validate_arr_url('http://2130706433/')` and `validate_arr_url('http://2852039166/')` return allowed, while `httpx.URL` preserves those hosts and the OS resolver maps them to `127.0.0.1` and `169.254.169.254`. Because saved URLs are later passed directly to `httpx.AsyncClient(base_url=...)`, this leaves a real loopback/cloud-metadata SSRF bypass despite the new IPv4-mapped IPv6 checks.
findings.fix_hint: Do not fall through blindly on IP parse failures. Canonicalize the host first, reject numeric-looking hosts that `ipaddress` cannot parse, or enforce the blocked-address policy on resolved socket addresses at request time. Add regression tests for forms such as `2130706433`, `127.1`, `0x7f000001`, `2852039166`, and `0xA9.0xFE.0xA9.0xFE`.
findings.why_it_matters: Non-canonical numeric host forms (decimal/octal/hex IPv4) parse as ValueError in ipaddress but resolve to loopback/metadata addresses at the OS level, so saved URLs reach blocked targets despite the new hardening.
source_window: if addr.is_link_local or addr.is_loopback or addr.is_unspecified or addr.is_multicast:
